files.SKILL.md:
---
name: deliberate-delivery
description: Generic execution style that favors clear requirements, concrete verification, and iterative correction based on observed behavior.
---

# SKILL — Deliberate Delivery

## Outcome Over Narrative
Work is complete when the required deliverable exists in the required form and passes the relevant checks. Investigation and reasoning are useful only if they are converted into output artifacts.

## Clarify the Contract Early
Before implementation, identify the required outputs, behavior expectations, and any stated constraints. If validation exists, treat it as a concrete signal of correctness and align outputs to those requirements.

## Build in Small, Verifiable Steps
Prefer short, focused changes over broad rewrites. Keep each step understandable and testable so failures can be traced to a specific decision.

## Validate from Real Evidence
Run available checks, read failure output fully, and correct root causes directly. Avoid speculative fixes that are not tied to observed evidence.

## Preserve Correctness Across Execution Paths
Implementations should remain reliable under normal execution and expected interruption/error paths. Ensure cleanup, state consistency, and output integrity are maintained.

## Avoid Assumption Drift
When results conflict with expectations, revisit assumptions and inspect the actual artifacts instead of refining the same guess repeatedly. Measured behavior is more reliable than intuition.

## Finish with Confidence
Declare completion only when outputs and checks agree. If uncertainty remains, document the blocker and the evidence collected so the next iteration starts from facts.
